codeReview.summary: The migration SQL files (001, 002, 003) are well-structured, idempotent, and cover all schema requirements from the feature description. The conversation_sessions table has all 13 required fields with correct types, FK constraints, RLS policies, and indexes. The 002 migration correctly handles the status lifecycle expansion with legacy values. However, the getOrCreateUser() implementation has a critical bug: using `ignoreDuplicates: true` with `.select('*').single()` will fail for existing users because PostgreSQL's ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING combined with RETURNING only returns newly inserted rows, not skipped conflict rows. The test suite (39 tests) is thorough for SQL structure validation but only verifies mock call patterns for getOrCreateUser, which masks the runtime bug.

issues.description: ignoreDuplicates: true causes getOrCreateUser() to fail for existing users. Supabase's upsert with ignoreDuplicates:true translates to PostgreSQL INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING. When combined with .select('*').single(), this returns zero rows for existing users (ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING + RETURNING * only returns newly inserted rows, not conflicting ones). The .single() call will throw a PGRST116 error ('The result contains 0 rows') on the second call for the same phone number. This directly violates the expectedBehavior: 'calling twice with same phone produces one row' — the first call succeeds but the second call throws. Fix: remove ignoreDuplicates:true (default false uses ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE SET which always returns the row), or use .maybeSingle() with a fallback select.

The 'upsert safety' test (line ~282) uses mocked Supabase that always returns the mock user regardless of actual Supabase behavior. The test verifies the mock was called with the right arguments but cannot catch the ignoreDuplicates runtime bug because mockQueryBuilder.single always resolves with the provided data. A more robust test would verify behavior with a real Supabase client or simulate the empty-return scenario.

Inconsistent RLS policy creation pattern. 001_initial.sql uses CREATE POLICY IF NOT EXISTS, while 003_conversation_sessions.sql uses DROP POLICY IF EXISTS followed by CREATE POLICY. Both approaches are idempotent but the inconsistency across migration files could confuse future contributors.

Adding 'node' to the project-wide tsconfig types array affects all compiled TypeScript, not just test files. A cleaner approach would be a separate tsconfig.test.json extending the base config, or using a triple-slash directive in the test file. This is minor since the agent already depends on @types/node and the project runs on Node.js.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker added 'node' to the project-wide tsconfig.json types array to support node:fs and node:path imports in test files. AGENTS.md doesn't mention conventions about tsconfig types management or whether test-specific types should be isolated to a separate tsconfig.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: agent/tsconfig.json line 20: types changed from ["vitest/globals"] to ["vitest/globals", "node"]. This was needed for agent/src/__tests__/db-schema-migration.test.ts which imports from node:fs and node:path.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker created 001_initial.sql as part of this feature even though the feature description and preconditions state '001_initial.sql exists' as a precondition. The worker had to create the foundational schema because no prior migration existed. This worked but means the feature did more than scoped — future features depending on 001 may not realize it was created here.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: features.json preconditions: ['vitest-setup feature is complete', 'supabase/migrations/001_initial.sql exists']. git show eb60a1d --stat shows 001_initial.sql was created (67 insertions).

sharedStateObservations.observation: Supabase upsert with ignoreDuplicates:true does not return conflicting rows — only newly inserted rows are returned via RETURNING. This is a PostgREST/PostgreSQL behavior that should be documented in the library to prevent other workers from making the same mistake. The correct pattern for 'get or create' is upsert without ignoreDuplicates (which does ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE SET) so the row is always returned.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: agent/src/db.ts:23-33 uses upsert with ignoreDuplicates:true + .select('*').single() — this will fail at runtime for existing rows.

summary: The db-schema-migration feature delivers well-structured SQL migrations (001_initial, 002_update_status_lifecycle, 003_conversation_sessions) that correctly implement the schema requirements: all 13 conversation_sessions fields, new status CHECK constraint with legacy values, timestamp columns, indexes, RLS policies, and idempotent patterns. However, getOrCreateUser() has a blocking bug — using ignoreDuplicates:true with .select('*').single() will throw for existing users because ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING + RETURNING only returns newly inserted rows. The test suite (39 tests) validates SQL structure thoroughly but masks this bug through mocking. Status: FAIL due to the blocking getOrCreateUser() issue.
